Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Paper states DREB26/ERF12 is an AP2/ERF transcription factor and Discussion places it in the A5 subfamily of AP2/ERF TFs.

Regulator_Type.reason: DREB26/ERF12 is explicitly described as an AP2/ERF TF/transcription factor.

Source_Species.reason: Study uses Arabidopsis and identifies DREB26/ERF12 in Arabidopsis.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Experimental procedures state Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Columbia was used.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Experimental procedures specify ecotype Columbia (Col-0; WT).

Applied_Species.reason: All core experiments were performed in Arabidopsis.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state DREB26/ERF12 expression transiently increased at 24 h then returned by 48 h; DREB26pro::GUS detected in cotyledons, true leaves, primary roots, ovules within carpels, early embryo and suspensor; GUS increased in whole leaves under dehydration; DREB26 promoter/GFP data support function in outer root/hypocotyl epidermal cells.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same Arabidopsis system as source species; all listed expression features are reported in Results.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: eGFP-fused DREB26 protein was detected mainly in the nucleus.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results repeatedly link DREB26 to increased/decreased very-long-chain alkanes, a major wax component.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: VLC alkanes are explicitly discussed as major cuticular wax components, i.e. lipids/cuticular wax.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Paper links wax/cuticle changes with reduced water loss, leaf permeability, and drought tolerance-related water retention.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Wax was measured on leaf surface and cuticle/epicuticular wax of aerial organs is described in the paper.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Results support regulation of cuticular wax biosynthesis, with especially strong effects on VLC-alkane accumulation and CER1/SHN3.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Protoplast assays showed DREB26 activates CER1 and SHN3 promoters, supporting transcriptional activation.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Figure 4A/Results state KCS2, CER26L, CER1, MAH, and SHN3 increased in DREB26OX; KCS2, CER26L, CER1, and SHN3 decreased in DREB26SR; KCS16 was negatively affected; Figure S7 text says cutin genes showed no clear changes.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: CER1 and SHN3 promoters were directly tested in protoplast transactivation assays and activated by DREB26.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: Drought/dehydration induction is explicit, but 'likely early ABA-associated drought response' is only indirectly inferred from an ABA increase in a previous dehydration system, not directly shown for DREB26 regulation here.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were used in Results for DREB26: overexpression, SRDX repression, qRT-PCR, GC-MS wax profiling, toluidine blue assay, TEM, water loss assay, and protoplast transactivation.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show DREB26OX increased wax and VLC-alkanes, thickened cuticle, and improved water content/reduced water loss; DREB26SR had opposite effects and increased permeability.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: This is an accurate synthesis of the DREB26 Results: early drought-inducible nuclear regulator, overexpression/repression effects on cuticle and wax, and activation of CER1/SHN3.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: Paper concludes DREB26 is an early drought-induced positive regulator initiating transcriptional regulation leading to VLC-alkane-rich cuticular wax accumulation.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Results use soil drought/dehydration by withholding watering; many assays were indeed under long-day conditions per growth methods unless otherwise noted.

Applied_Promoters.reason: 35S promoter was used for overexpression/repression and GFP fusions; native DREB26 promoter was used for GUS/GFP reporter assays.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results show fewer rosette leaves and earlier flowering in DREB26OX; DREB26SR showed growth retardation, chlorosis, anthocyanin accumulation, leaf adhesion, and late flowering.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Utility for engineering drought tolerance is a reasonable inference from Results, but the recommendation for stress-inducible or epidermis-specific deployment is not stated in the paper.

Omics_Data.reason: Supporting Information lists microarray analysis in Figures S4 and S5; no accession is provided, so 'accession NA' is consistent with absence of accession.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results/discussion support relative specificity toward VLC-alkane biosynthesis over cutin genes, and Discussion explicitly notes stronger DREB26SR phenotype may reflect suppression of additional related AP2/ERFs.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: ERF13 is described as an AP2/ERF TF, and Discussion places DREB26/ERF12, ERF13, and ERF14 in the A5 subfamily.

Regulator_Type.reason: ERF13 is explicitly a transcription factor.

Source_Species.reason: ERF13 studied here is from Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is the experimental species.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state ERF13 transiently increased at 24 h then returned by 48 h; ERF13pro::GUS detected in cotyledons, true leaves, primary roots, and petals; leaf GUS increased under dehydration.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same Arabidopsis system; the listed expression pattern is reported in Results.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: eGFP-fused ERF13 was detected mainly in the nucleus.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Through the erf12/13/14 triple mutant and promoter activation assays, ERF13 is linked to drought-responsive VLC-alkane accumulation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: VLC alkanes are described as major cuticular wax lipids.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Paper links wax/cuticle changes with lower water loss and better leaf water content under drought.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Wax metabolites were measured on leaf surface; cuticle/epicuticular wax on aerial organs is described.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: ERF13 is part of the DREB26/ERF12-ERF13-ERF14 module regulating cuticular wax biosynthesis, especially affecting VLC-alkane accumulation in triple mutant data.

Regulation_Mode.reason: ERF13 activated CER1 and SHN3 promoters in protoplast assays, supporting transcriptional activation.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Direct activation of CER1 and SHN3 is shown; contribution to drought induction of wax genes is supported by reduced expression of some wax genes in the triple mutant.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: CER1 and SHN3 promoters were directly activated by ERF13 in Figure 4C.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: ERF13 expression was induced by drought/dehydration stress.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are used for ERF13-related evidence: promoter::GUS, GFP localization, protoplast assays, CRISPR-based mutant generation contributing to triple mutant, qRT-PCR and GC-MS analyses in triple mutant.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Individual ERF13 phenotype for wax was not resolved, but Results support redundant action with DREB26/ERF12 and ERF14 in promoting drought-induced VLC-alkane accumulation and leaf water retention via triple mutant data.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: This summary matches the evidence presented for ERF13 as an early drought-inducible nuclear AP2/ERF that activates CER1/SHN3 and contributes redundantly in the triple mutant.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: ERF13 is supported as a redundant co-activator within the A5 AP2/ERF module for drought-responsive wax production.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Drought/dehydration by withholding watering is the treatment used.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Native ERF13 promoter was used for GUS, and 35S promoter was used for GFP fusion and effector constructs in transactivation assays.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Triple mutant shows growth reduction under drought, but the specific contribution of ERF13 alone to tradeoffs is not resolved in Results.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Potential engineering value is inferential and not directly discussed for ERF13 specifically.

Omics_Data.reason: Supporting information mentions microarray analyses; no accession is given.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion explicitly notes a prior report that ERF13 functions in VLC-FA biosynthesis during lateral root development, indicating pleiotropy.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: ERF14 is described as an AP2/ERF transcription factor and included in the A5 subfamily in Discussion.

Source_Species.reason: ERF14 studied here is from Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species.reason: Core work was done in Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is stated in methods.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state ERF14 transcript transiently increased at 24 h and returned by 48 h; ERF14pro::GUS was detected in roots but not leaves; not clearly changed under dehydration; reproductive expression detected in immature stamen in buds.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same Arabidopsis system and expression pattern are directly reported.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: eGFP-fused ERF14 protein was mainly nuclear.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Through triple mutant analyses and promoter activation assays, ERF14 is linked to drought-responsive VLC-alkane accumulation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: VLC alkanes are major cuticular wax lipids in the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Paper associates wax/cuticle regulation with reduced non-stomatal water loss and drought-related water retention.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Wax was measured on leaf surface cuticle/epicuticular wax of aerial organs.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: ERF14 is included with DREB26/ERF12 and ERF13 in regulation of cuticular wax biosynthesis, especially drought-induced VLC-alkanes.

Regulation_Mode.reason: ERF14 activated CER1 and SHN3 promoters in protoplast assays.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Direct activation of CER1 and SHN3 is shown, and triple mutant data support contribution to induction of wax genes under drought.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: CER1 and SHN3 were directly tested and activated by ERF14.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: ERF14 transcript induction by drought/dehydration is shown, albeit weaker in promoter-GUS leaves.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Promoter::GUS, GFP localization, protoplast transactivation, CRISPR/Cas9 mutant generation, and triple-mutant qRT-PCR/GC-MS analyses are all described.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Individual ERF14 effect is not isolated, but redundant contribution with DREB26/ERF12 and ERF13 to VLC-alkane accumulation and leaf water maintenance is supported by triple mutant results.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: This summary accurately reflects the evidence: drought-responsive nuclear AP2/ERF with root/reproductive expression, activates CER1/SHN3, and contributes redundantly in the triple mutant.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: ERF14 is supported as a redundant co-regulator in the A5 AP2/ERF module for drought-induced cuticular wax synthesis.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Soil drought/dehydration by withholding watering is the experimental context.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Native ERF14 promoter for GUS and 35S promoter for GFP fusion/effector constructs are described.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Only triple mutant growth phenotype is shown; ERF14-specific tradeoffs are not individually resolved.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Potential engineering value is not directly stated for ERF14 and is inferential.

Omics_Data.reason: Supporting information mentions microarray analyses; no accession is provided.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion explicitly states ERF14 has reported roles in fungal defense gene regulation, supporting broader pleiotropy.
